text.title: ICP Fit Scorecard Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in solo ICP-scoring runs. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix keyed to a methodology rule.

antipattern.symptom: Histogram shows 80% in KEEP after the first run; founder feels validated; no removes triggered.
antipattern.root-cause: Signals scored generously to avoid the cognitive cost of removing real customers.
antipattern.fix: Lock signal maxima per r1. Audit a sample of 10 rows against evidence per r3. If KEEP > 50% of book, re-score with stricter evidence threshold.

antipattern.symptom: REMOVE list generated but no sunset emails sent 60 days later; same customers re-appear in next quarter's REMOVE list.
antipattern.root-cause: Scorecard treated as informational only; no calendar trigger.
antipattern.fix: Every REMOVE row gets a due_date within 30 days. Calendar reminder + pre-commit hook flags overdue removes. Rule r5.

antipattern.symptom: Validator passes but signal scores look identical across half the book; evidence notes are generic ("seems like a fit").
antipattern.root-cause: Operator filled evidence with placeholder text.
antipattern.fix: Reject rows where evidence per signal is < 5 chars or matches a regex of known placeholders. Rule r3.

antipattern.symptom: Quarterly re-score skipped; founder uses 9-month-old scorecard for a price-change decision.
antipattern.root-cause: No calendar entry; "I'll do it next month".
antipattern.fix: Pre-commit hook fails on validator if scored_at > 100 days old. Force quarterly cadence. Rule r4.
